idea_text: I will binary search the maximum x. The check is 5*x*(x+1)/2 <= 240-k and x <= n.
I can binary search the integer root r and compare r*r with the sum to avoid floating point sqrt.
I will precompute triangular numbers and for each value binary search if n minus it is also triangular.
I want to solve the quadratic formula for x^2+x-2*T <= 0 and use sqrt(1+8*T).
I compute the integer sqrt of the total sum and check root*root == sum as the formula condition.
For each triangular t, I test n-t with the discriminant 1+8*m being a perfect square.
